overview.introduction: Jenkins is an open-source automation server that orchestrates software delivery tasks.
overview.why_it_matters: Understanding Jenkins Fundamentals allows developers to automate release iterations safely and predictably.
overview.industry_usage: Adopted by enterprise companies to scale integration and delivery jobs workloads.
overview.what_you_will_build: A configured local Git repository with a custom Jenkinsfile running inside the Jenkins logs simulator.
learning_outcomes: Explain the purpose and mechanics of Jenkins Fundamentals.
Incorporate validation checks like linting, unit tests, and compilation checks.
Incorporate credentials, agent labeling parameters, and option attributes.
Analyze and debug running pipeline build logs.

key_concepts.title: Automation Server Architecture
key_concepts.definition: Jenkins automates compile, test, and release cycles using jobs and pipelines.
key_concepts.explanation: A Jenkins pipeline splits software delivery tasks into sequential steps, ensuring validation gates are satisfied.

commands.name: jenkins build
commands.purpose: Simulate and run Jenkinsfile pipeline stages.
commands.output_explanation: Executes local stages configured inside Jenkinsfile.

key_concepts.pro_tip: Keep Jenkinsfiles modular to easily pinpoint error locations and optimize resource utilization.
key_concepts.best_practices: Always archive build artifacts and run test stages early in the pipeline timeline.
key_concepts.common_mistakes: Typing stage names or parentheses incorrectly in Groovy syntaxes.
key_concepts.real_world_usage: Jenkins pipelines compile code, run tests, and deploy packages for every developer push command.
key_concepts.summary: Jenkins Fundamentals forms the foundation of enterprise CI/CD automation.

interactive_examples.common_mistakes: Typing brackets incorrectly in Groovy syntax definitions.

lessons.title: 1. Create Configuration (Create initial Jenkinsfile)
lessons.instruction: Create a file named 'Jenkinsfile' in the root directory to define your stages list.

lessons.title: 2. Inspect Status
lessons.instruction: Run git status to see modified files.

lessons.title: 3. Audit History Log
lessons.explanation: Displays timeline log of previous commits.
lessons.instruction: Print commit history log to console screen.

lessons.title: 5. Show pointer references
lessons.explanation: Locates branches and tags SHA mappings.
lessons.instruction: Query refs mapping by running git show-ref.

lessons.title: 6. Show active branches
lessons.instruction: List all branches using git branch.

lessons.title: Mini Challenge: Jenkins Setup
lessons.definition: No step-by-step instructions are provided here. Execute this task directly.
lessons.explanation: Complete this task on your own.
lessons.instruction: Construct a basic pipeline schema in 'Jenkinsfile' listing the core 'Build' stage.

exercises.title: Configure local Jenkinsfile
exercises.problem: Practice Jenkinsfile configuration by configuring Jenkinsfile.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of Jenkins Jenkins Fundamentals?
options: To automate software integration and delivery pipelines.
To assign cloud infrastructure access permissions.
To coordinate cluster pods networking schedules.
quiz.answer: To automate software integration and delivery pipelines.
quiz.explanation: Jenkins is an automation server that compiles, tests, and deploys code.

quiz.question: What is Pipeline as Code in Jenkins?
options: Defining build stages and pipeline flows using a Jenkinsfile in version control.
Writing raw machine compiler bytecode instructions.
Manually clicking configuration buttons in the UI dashboard.
quiz.answer: Defining build stages and pipeline flows using a Jenkinsfile in version control.
quiz.explanation: Pipeline as Code allows defining Jenkins pipelines in a text file (Jenkinsfile) committed to git.

quiz.question: What distinguishes Declarative from Scripted pipelines in Jenkins?
options: Declarative pipelines use a structured, pre-defined syntax with 'pipeline' block.
Scripted pipelines use strictly XML elements.
Declarative pipelines run no unit tests.
Scripted pipelines do not support parallel stages.
quiz.answer: Declarative pipelines use a structured, pre-defined syntax with 'pipeline' block.
quiz.explanation: Declarative syntax offers a clean, opinionated layout with structure checks, while Scripted uses raw imperative Groovy code.

quiz.question: What block contains all the stage definitions in a Declarative pipeline?
quiz.explanation: The 'stages' block contains the individual 'stage' definitions in a Declarative pipeline layout.

quiz.question: How do you specify where a stage or pipeline should execute in Jenkins?
quiz.explanation: The 'agent' parameter specifies where the build steps will execute (e.g. 'agent any' or a specific label worker agent).

quiz.question: Which block encapsulates actual execution commands inside a stage?
quiz.explanation: The 'steps' block is nested inside a stage and lists the sequential build command instructions.

quiz.question: What utility securely binds API keys and SSH logins in pipelines?
quiz.explanation: The credentials() wrapper links defined secret keys and tokens safely inside environment variables without printing them.

quiz.question: What option prunes old pipeline build logs and histories?
quiz.explanation: The buildDiscarder option limits the number of historical build logs stored on the server disk.

quiz.question: What block executes actions based on pipeline success or failure outcomes?
quiz.explanation: The 'post' block defines condition actions (such as success, failure, always) that execute after the stages complete.

quiz.question: What is the file name of the standard pipeline config committed in Git?
quiz.explanation: Jenkinsfile (without extension) is the default configuration file for defining Jenkins pipelines.

resources.summary: Learn more about Jenkins Jenkins Fundamentals through official documentation, guides, and cheat sheets.

links.title: Jenkins Jenkins Fundamentals Guide
links.description: Official documentation and manual for Jenkins Fundamentals.

links.title: Jenkins Pipeline Syntax
links.description: Standard references and specifications for Jenkins pipeline layouts.

revision_notes: Pipelines are written as declarative or scripted syntax inside Jenkinsfile.
Build discarders option prevents server disk utilization overload.
Agents distribute jobs execution loads away from the master controller.
best_practices: Always run pipelines inside version controlled Jenkinsfile.
Prune build history logs using buildDiscarder options.
Never store plain credentials tokens inside scripts.
beginner_mistakes: Omitting closing brackets in Groovy pipeline scripts.

interview_questions.question: What is Jenkins Jenkins Fundamentals?
interview_questions.answer: It is an automation server delivering repeatable, automated code pipelines.

books.title: Jenkins 2: Up and Running — Brent Laster
books.description: The definitive book explaining Jenkins pipelines as code, agents configurations, and integrations.
